examples: I may look like a tenderfoot, but I think I can follow a trail or carry a pack if necessary.
I guess he is a business tenderfoot.
I served the first part of my sentence in several correctional work camps of mixed types (this kind of camp is described in the play, the Tenderfoot and the Tramp).
The movie alternates predictable tenderfoot gags (sore behinds, stampedes, and the like) with long, ludicrous passages of group-therapy-on-the-range.
